behaviors.description: An image containing explicit content is flagged and held for review, not published.
behaviors.justification: Failure risk is model-quality dependent (LLM-DEP): a mocked classifier can’t reveal real misclassification of explicit imagery. Decision routing (hold vs publish) can be checked in Integration, but the real failure boundary here is whether the vision model actually flags explicit content.
behaviors.test_description: Run moderation with a real vision model on a curated explicit-image set, then assert each submission is held-for-review and not published.

behaviors.description: A text post containing hate speech is flagged regardless of obfuscation techniques (e.g., letter substitution, unicode lookalikes).
behaviors.justification: This is robustness/quality of real model understanding under adversarial text variants (LLM-DEP). Mocking text classification would likely hide the true failure mode (MOCK-HIDE).
behaviors.test_description: Submit real obfuscated hate-speech variants to the production-equivalent moderation path using the real text model; assert all are flagged and held.

behaviors.description: A post that passes both content checks is published rather than held or silently dropped.
behaviors.justification: Primary risk is module handoff/decision wiring (MOCK-MISS): safe outputs from both classifiers must propagate correctly to publish state. Real model quality is not the target of this behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Wire moderation service + persistence/queue + publisher with classifier stubs returning 'safe'; assert final state is published, not held, and not missing.

behaviors.description: The full moderation pipeline — image check, text check, publish decision — completes within 5 seconds of submission.
behaviors.justification: This is end-to-end latency across multiple real agentic steps in sequence (MULTI-STEP + LLM-DEP), plus orchestration overhead. Lower-level tests can miss cumulative timing and contention effects.
behaviors.test_description: Execute full submission flow (real image model + real text model + decision path) under peak-like load and measure p95/p99 completion; assert SLA <= 5s.

behaviors.description: When the AI vision model is unavailable, image posts are queued for retry rather than auto-approved.
behaviors.justification: Depends on real dependency failure behavior (REAL-INFRA): timeouts/network errors/service unavailability and queueing semantics. Mocked failures can miss real retry/timeout edge cases (MOCK-HIDE).
behaviors.test_description: Induce real vision-model unavailability (service down/network block) and submit image posts; assert posts enter retry queue and never transition to auto-approved.

behaviors.description: The moderation decision for a borderline image is consistent when the same image is submitted multiple times within a short window.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly targets consistency under model uncertainty/non-determinism (LLM-DEP). Repeated real calls are needed to catch variance; mocks would mask this.
behaviors.test_description: Submit the same borderline image repeatedly within the target window through real moderation, then assert decision consistency policy (e.g., identical outcome or stable cached outcome) is met.
